Sarah Chen

Portfolio dashboard

Logged in as main (jonathan@dimagi.com) · http://localhost:8000/funder/

Sarah sees every funder relationship at a glance — KPIs, budget, and linked program count on a single scroll-free page

✨ AI Quality ★★★☆☆ (3/5)

Overall: 3/5. A: Content 3/5 — 2 of 3 funds show 'No budget set'; missing aggregate Total Budget KPI. B: App Page 4/5 — clean 3-card grid, green Active badges. C: Screenshot 4/5. D: Slide 4/5 — narration 'at a glance' validated. E: Demo Ready 3/5 — 'No budget set' is the first thing a funder would ask about. Fix: [DATA] set budgets on Founders Pledge and Gavi; [CODE] add aggregate 'Total Portfolio Budget' KPI to the top row.

Sarah Chen

Fund detail — KPIs & impact

Logged in as main (jonathan@dimagi.com) · http://localhost:8000/funder/fund/2268/

KPIs stream in live from Connect API — no manual export, no dashboard refresh lag

✨ AI Quality ★★★☆☆ (3/5)

Overall: 3/5. A: Content 4/5 — real impact headline: 'Your fund has reached 31,423 families across 1 country through 80 community health workers'. B: App Page 3/5 — Total Budget shows '—' and Remaining still shows 'loading...' even after SSE complete (likely a bug where remaining depends on budget). C: Screenshot 4/5. D: Slide 3/5 — narration 'KPIs stream in live' undercut by 'loading...' still visible. E: Demo Ready 3/5. Fix: [CODE] when total_budget is not set, hide the Remaining card entirely rather than showing 'loading...' forever. [DATA] set total_budget on Gavi fund.

Sarah Chen

Performance table

Logged in as main (jonathan@dimagi.com) · http://localhost:8000/funder/fund/2268/

Ranked by live activity — Sarah instantly sees which grantees are on track and which need a check-in

✨ AI Quality ★★★☆☆ (3/5)

Overall: 3/5. A: Content 4/5 — real data: ISODAF at 30,999 visits / $24,318; ZEGCAWIS with a stale-delivery alert. B: App Page 4/5 — clean table with trend indicators. C: Screenshot 4/5. D: Slide 4/5 — narration validated by the alert banner. E: Demo Ready 3/5 — 'no visits in 138 days' alert is honest data but projects poorly in a demo. Fix: [DATA] seed a third more-active allocation so the table isn't dominated by one stale opportunity.

Sarah Chen

Activity + Payments charts

Logged in as main (jonathan@dimagi.com) · http://localhost:8000/funder/fund/2268/

Cross-chart filtering lets Sarah explore delivery data like a dashboard, not a static report

✨ AI Quality ★★★☆☆ (3/5)

Overall: 3/5. A: Content 3/5 — one opportunity shows green bars (active delivery), the other shows near-zero throughout. B: App Page 4/5 — Chart.js renders are clean, side-by-side layout works well. C: Screenshot 4/5. D: Slide 3/5 — narration promises 'cross-chart filtering' but filter controls aren't visible in this view. E: Demo Ready 3/5 — charts look sparse because only one opp has meaningful data. Fix: [DATA] add more active allocations; [SLIDE] update narration or capture a scene WITH filters applied.

Sarah Chen

Delivery pace forecast

Logged in as main (jonathan@dimagi.com) · http://localhost:8000/funder/fund/2268/

Positive framing — delivery pace as impact delivered, not burn rate

✨ AI Quality ★★★☆☆ (3/5)

Overall: 3/5. A: Content 3/5 — headline '$48,466 total impact delivered' is positive BUT subheading shows '-$6,503 per week delivery pace' — negative sign breaks the positive framing. B: App Page 4/5 — clean Chart.js line chart with dashed projection line. C: Screenshot 4/5. D: Slide 3/5 — narration 'positive framing' contradicted by negative dollar figure in UI. E: Demo Ready 3/5. Fix: [CODE] remove the negative sign on 'per week delivery pace' label; the rate should always display as a positive weekly velocity.

Issues Found

  • ⚠ Scene 1: 2 of 3 funds show 'No budget set' — missing data on the portfolio overview.
  • ⚠ Scene 2: Remaining KPI shows 'loading...' indefinitely when total_budget is not set — should hide the card instead.
  • ⚠ Scene 6: '-$6,503 per week delivery pace' uses negative sign, contradicts the 'positive framing' design intent.
  • ✗ Scene 7: AI Impact Report teased but not demonstrated — clicking Generate Report would produce a much stronger scene.
  • ✗ Scene 8: Export PDF narration not matched by capture — would need to trigger print preview.
